description: Goal: make compiler2's fact engine behave like an efficient spreadsheet: jobs read the minimum current evidence needed to make progress, wait only for settled evidence where correctness requires it, and identify semantic work by intentional activation partitions rather than accidental raw type snapshots. Signal: engine tests prove current/settled readiness; semantic tests prove ActivationInputs is the body-evidence authority; quicksort/Enum.reduce compile through a small measured frontier; ReturnEvidence distinguishes missing, unknown, no-return, and known evidence; any/none appear only as real domain types. Strategy: repair the engine first, split activation identity from input evidence, install policy-driven activation partition keys, then replace ReturnType with ReturnEvidence and burn down remaining any/none poison from the earliest wrong fact. Outro: grep-level deletion contract proves ActivationKey-as-body-input, recursive-only key widening, list-only convergence_class, raw closure-capture key defaults, missing-return-as-none, unresolved-as-any, ReturnType-as-ready-Ty, DependencySnapshot freshness, semantic_closure_is_current, and debug-payload telemetry budgets are gone rather than renamed.
title: Compiler2 fact readiness: current vs settled evidence
